title: Braised Chicken Drumsticks

instruction.title: Marinate the Chicken
steps: Mix marinade ingredients in a bowl, coat the drumsticks evenly.
Marinate for at least 30 minutes (overnight in the fridge for deeper flavor).

instruction.title: Brown the Chicken
steps: Heat vegetable oil in a heavy skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add drumsticks and sear until golden brown on all sides (about 3–4 minutes per side). Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: Build the Braising Base
steps: In the same pan, add ginger, garlic, and green onion. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add sugar and let it melt slightly to form a light caramel.

instruction.title: Braise the Chicken
steps: Add light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, and water/chicken stock.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to low heat. Cover and simmer for 25–30 minutes, turning occasionally.

instruction.title: Finish and Glaze
steps: Uncover, turn heat to medium-high, and reduce the sauce until it becomes thick and glossy, coating the drumsticks.
Drizzle with sesame oil for aroma.

instruction.title: Plate and Serve
steps: Arrange chicken on a serving plate.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds.
Serve hot with steamed rice or stir-fried vegetables.

chefTips: Use bone-in chicken: Gives better flavor and moistness.
Caramelize sugar properly: Adds depth of color and a subtle sweetness.
Low and slow braising: Ensures tender, juicy meat.
Finish with sesame oil: Adds that signature Chinese aroma.
Optional: Add a few dried chili peppers or star anise during braising for aromatic complexity.
